title: USS New Orleans Patrols Arabian Sea as US Naval Blockade of Iran Enters Third Week
summary: USS New Orleans (LPD 18) conducts patrols in the Arabian Sea supporting the naval blockade of Iranian ports, with 48 commercial vessels redirected over 20 days.
contents:
## USS New Orleans Arabian Sea Patrol Update

### Current Operations
US Central Command confirmed that USS New Orleans (LPD 18), a San Antonio-class amphibious transport dock, currently patrols the Arabian Sea in support of the ongoing naval blockade of Iranian ports. The vessel operates as part of the broader US naval force maintaining pressure on Tehran's maritime capabilities.

### Blockade Statistics
Over the past 20 days since the blockade commenced on April 13, US naval forces redirected 48 commercial vessels to ensure compliance with blockade restrictions. US officials estimate the economic squeeze costs Iran approximately $170 million per day in lost oil revenue.

### Iranian Response
Iran started cutting oil production as storage facilities near Kharg Island approached capacity. Tehran increasingly relies on floating storage, aging tankers, and ship-to-ship transfers to maintain crude exports. The IRGC Navy announced "new rules" governing waters near Iran's southern coastline, asserting control over approximately 2,000 kilometers of coastline across the Arabian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz.

### Force Posture
Following the USS Gerald R Ford carrier strike group's departure, two carrier groups remain in the region — the USS Abraham Lincoln and USS George HW Bush — among approximately 20 US ships. The 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit reportedly operates near the southern Philippines while the 22nd MEU participates in Operation Southern Spear and the 31st MEU supports Operation Epic Fury.
